query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Magnate', 'A person of great influence in a particular field', 'Critics argued that the plan might magnate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Magnet', 'A thing or person that attracts', 'Her magnet became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Magnetize', 'To make magnetic', 'Critics argued that the plan might magnetize the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Magnificence', 'Grandeur, brilliance, lavishness or splendor', 'Her magnificence became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Magnify', 'To make something appear larger than it is', 'Over time, the community learned to magnify its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Magniloquent', 'Speaking pompously, using swelling discourse, bombastic', 'What seemed magniloquent at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Magnitude', 'Size, extent, dimensions', 'Scholars noted a striking magnitude between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Maharaja', 'A ruling prince in india, especially one of major states', 'The essay highlights the maharaja of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Maidenhood', 'The time when one is a maiden or young girl', 'The essay highlights the maidenhood of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Maize', 'Corn, a type of grain of the species zea mays', 'The author hoped the new policy would maize tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Maladroit', 'Unskillful, awkward, bungling', 'Scholars noted a striking maladroit between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Malady', 'Any ailment or disease of the human body', 'Her malady became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Malaise', 'A feeling of general bodily discomfort or unpleasantness', 'The essay highlights the malaise of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Malapropism', 'The use of an inappropriate word in place of a similar one', 'Scholars noted a striking malapropism between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Malaria', 'Disease characterized by chills, fever, and sweating', 'Her malaria became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Malcontent', 'Dissatisfied with current conditions, discontented', 'What seemed malcontent at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Malediction', 'A curse, imprecation, slander', 'The essay highlights the malediction of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Malefactor', 'A criminal or felon', 'Her malefactor became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Maleficent', 'Harmful or evil in intent or effect', 'What seemed maleficent at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Malevolent', 'Having or showing a wish to do evil to others', 'Over time, the community learned to malevolent its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Malfeasance', 'An act by a public official that is contrary to law', 'The essay highlights the malfeasance of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Malicious', 'Of, pertaining to, or as a result of malice or spite', 'What seemed malicious at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Malign', 'Say harmful things about', 'The essay highlights the malign of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Malignant', 'Disposed to cause harm, suffering, or distress deliberately', 'The essay highlights the malignant of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Malinger', 'To pretend illness, especially in order to shirk one''s duty', 'Scholars noted a striking malinger between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Malingerer', 'A person who malingers', 'Scholars noted a striking malingerer between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Malleable', 'Easily shaped, influenced, or adaptable', '“Gold is highly malleable and can be hammered into thin sheets.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Mallet', 'A hammerlike tool with a head commonly of wood', 'The essay highlights the mallet of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Maltreat', 'To treat badly', 'Critics argued that the plan might maltreat the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Mandate', 'An authoritative command', 'The essay highlights the mandate of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Mane', 'The long hair growing on the neck of some animals', 'Her mane became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Maneuver', 'To move (something) carefully, and often with difficulty', 'The author hoped the new policy would maneuver tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Mania', 'Violent derangement of mind, madness, insanity', 'The essay highlights the mania of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Manifest', '', 'The essay highlights the manifest of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Manifestation', 'An outward or visible expression of something', '“The rising tensions were a manifestation of deeper social issues.”', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Manifold', 'Diverse, varied', 'The essay highlights the manifold of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Manipulatable', 'Capable of being manipulated', 'The passage presents a manipulatable portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Mannerism', 'A group of habitual behaviors peculiar to an individual', 'Critics argued that the plan might mannerism the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Manor', 'A landed estate', 'Scholars noted a striking manor between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Mantel', 'The shelf above a fireplace', 'The essay highlights the mantel of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Mantle', 'To cover or conceal (something)', 'The author hoped the new policy would mantle tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Manufacturer', 'A company that owns or runs a manufacturing plant', 'Scholars noted a striking manufacturer between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Manumission', 'Release from slavery, freedom, the act of manumitting', 'Her manumission became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Manumit', 'To release from slavery, to free', 'Critics argued that the plan might manumit the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Mar', 'To damage or spoil, render less perfect, attractive', 'Over time, the community learned to mar its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Maraud', 'To roam or go around in quest of plunder', 'Scholars noted a striking maraud between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Marauder', 'Someone who moves about looking for plunder', 'The essay highlights the marauder of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Marginal', 'Of, relating to, or located at a margin or an edge', 'Her marginal became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Marine', 'Pertaining to the sea (marine biology, marine insurance)', 'Critics argued that the plan might marine the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Maritime', 'Related to the sea or sailing', 'Critics argued that the plan might maritime the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
